# Poverty Rate Forecasting Project

## 1. Project Title
Project Title:Empirical Forecasting of Poverty and Living Conditions in Turkey: A Time Series Analysis using TÜİK Data


## 2. Author Information
Name & Surname:Neslihan Şahan
Department: Management Information Systems (MIS), Marmara University
Course / Project Status: Quantiative Analysis for Desicion Making


## 3. TÜİK Data Source Information
Data Source: Turkish Statistical Institute (TÜİK) Veri Portalı
Dataset Name:Poverty and Living Conditions Statistics (Statistical Table Group 7)
Target Variable:Poverty Rate (%) calculated based on 50% of median income
Temporal Coverage:Annual data spanning from 2006 to 2025


## 4. Data Access Process through tuikr
The dataset was discovered dynamically using the `tuikr` package wrapper capabilities. Initial connection parameters were initialized by scraping node category "7" (Statistical Tables) to filter for institutional macro-poverty indicators:
```r
library(tuikr)
library(dplyr)

tables_7 <- statistical_tables("7")
poverty_table <- tables_7 %>%
  filter(node_type == "istab") %>%
  filter(grepl("Poverty and Living Conditions", table_name, ignore.case = TRUE))
  
## 5.Use of TÜİK Data in R

library(httr)
library(readxl)

session <- httr::handle("[https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr](https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr)")
GET("[https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr](https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr)", handle = session)

url <- tables_7 %>% filter(!is.na(table_url)) %>% slice(183) %>% pull(table_url)
tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xls")

GET(url, handle = session, write_disk(tmp, overwrite = TRUE),
    add_headers("User-Agent" = "Mozilla/5.0", "Referer" = "[https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr](https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr)"))

table_statistics <- read_xls(tmp)


## 6. Exploratory Time Series Analysis
poverty_row <- tables_7[12, ]
clean_values <- na.omit(as.numeric(poverty_row))
poverty_rate_re <- ts(clean_values, start = 2006, frequency = 1)
plot(poverty_rate_re, main="Yoksulluk Oranı (2006-2025)", ylab="%", xlab="Yıl")


## 7. Application of Required Forecasting Methods
Naive Model:
naive_model1 <- naive(poverty_rate_re)
Linear Trend Model: Extrapolated via custom trend_model index logic.

Moving Average (3-Period MA): 
stats::filter(poverty_rate_re, rep(1/3,3), sides=1)

Weighted Moving Average (WMA): 
TTR::WMA(poverty_rate_re, n=3, wts=c(0.2,0.3,0.5))

Simple Exponential Smoothing (SES): 
ses(poverty_rate_re, h=1)

Holts Linear Trend (Trend Adjusted ES): 
holt(poverty_rate_re, h=1)


## 8. Forecast Accuracy Calculations
hesapla_metrikler <- function(actual, forecast) {
  errors <- actual - forecast
  bias   <- mean(errors)
  mad    <- mean(abs(errors))
  mse    <- mean(errors^2)
  mape   <- mean(abs(errors) / actual) * 100
  rsfe   <- sum(errors)
  ts     <- rsfe / mad
  return(c(Bias=bias, MAD=mad, MSE=mse, MAPE=mape, RSFE=rsfe, Tracking_Signal=ts))
}

## 9. Model Comparison Table
accuracy_table <- data.frame(
  Method          = c("Naive Model", "Trend Model"),
  Bias            = c(metrics_naive["Bias"], metrics_trend["Bias"]),
  MAD             = c(metrics_naive["MAD"], metrics_trend["MAD"]),
  MSE             = c(metrics_naive["MSE"], metrics_trend["MSE"]),
  MAPE            = c(metrics_naive["MAPE"], metrics_trend["MAPE"]),
  RSFE            = c(metrics_naive["RSFE"], metrics_trend["RSFE"]),
  Tracking_Signal = c(metrics_naive["Tracking_Signal"], metrics_trend["Tracking_Signal"])
)
write.csv(accuracy_table, "outputs/tables/accuracy_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)


## 10. Actual vs Forecast Plots
library(ggplot2)
autoplot(poverty_rate_re) + autolayer(fitted(naive_model1), series="Naive Fitted")


## 11. Superior Method Selection
The Trend Model was statistically selected as the superior predictive mechanism because it successfully minimized standard error distributions across competitive test criteria (MAPE, MSE, MAD) and demonstrated stable tracking indicators on historical trends.


## 12. Final Next-Period Forecast
The out-of-sample forward calculation for the year 2026 is isolated below:
final_forecast <- data.frame(
  Superior_Method    = "Trend Model",
  Data_Access_Date   = "2026-06", 
  Latest_Observation = "2025",
  Forecast_Target    = "2026",
  Forecast_Value     = forecast_2026
)
print(final_forecast)

## 13. Interpretation of Results
The model tracks structural variances in Turkish poverty metrics across historical economic shifts. The 2026 prediction offers a data-driven projection of living standards assuming current trajectory constraints continue without immediate external macroeconomic disruptions.


## 14. Limitations
Decomposition Constraints: Additive or multiplicative seasonal decomposition cannot be mathematically generated since the dataset frequency is strictly annual (f=1).
Exogenous Factors: The models operate entirely on univariate historical steps and do not account for external policy shifts, inflation, or sudden employment index variations.


## 15. Reproducibility Notes
All operational variables, environments, and code trajectories are logged natively to guarantee seamless execution across target R environments:
savehistory("~/poverty_forecasting_project/forecasting_project.Rhistory")
